purpose: Route non-trivial software feature delivery requests to harness-design (for orchestration), contract-review (for current-slice approval), frontend-evaluator (for independent QA), or compound (for structured knowledge extraction).
selection_order: Cross-session delivery-control design beats execution when the real question is how work should be orchestrated across sessions, handoffs, and QA loops.
Current-slice contract approval beats implementation when the proposed slice must be approved or rejected before execution starts.
Independent frontend acceptance beats builder-side web work when the browser-facing deliverable already exists and needs skeptical signoff rather than implementation.
Structured knowledge extraction beats ad-hoc notes when a feature or phase has completed and durable lessons or truths should be captured into docs/reference/ before context is lost.

children.name: harness-design
children.summary: Designs delivery-control rules for single-session work, compacted continuation, or planner/generator/evaluator loops so cross-session execution stays honest and bounded.
route_when: The request is about multi-session execution, delivery control, harness design, planner/generator/evaluator loops, or handoff contracts rather than product implementation.
The main need is to decide session boundaries, pass/fail gates, goal lineage, or required artifacts for work that will span resets, compaction, or multiple agents.
avoid_when: The user mainly wants the current slice approved or rejected, the product built, or defects fixed rather than the control model defined.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this nested child path before routing when the runtime cannot discover delivery-control children automatically.

children.name: contract-review
children.summary: Approves or rejects the current implementation slice before execution by reviewing the canonical contract, allowed boundary, and observable acceptance conditions.
route_when: The work already has a proposed implementation slice and the main need is deciding whether execution may start.
The user wants approval or rejection of the current slice contract rather than cross-session mode design, implementation, or post-build QA.
The authoritative slice contract lives in `docs/live/contract.md` or is expected to live there.
avoid_when: The main need is generic architecture review, product planning, code review after implementation, or browser QA after work already shipped.
The slice still needs orchestration design because session mode, baton rules, or return paths are not defined yet.
requires: A current implementation-slice contract that can be reviewed at `docs/live/contract.md`, or a clear instruction that the review is rejecting execution until that artifact exists.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this nested child path before routing when the runtime cannot discover delivery-control children automatically.

children.name: frontend-evaluator
children.summary: Runs independent browser-facing acceptance on an already-built frontend deliverable and returns a pass, fail, or blocked verdict with evidence, defects, and retry guidance.
route_when: The browser-facing work already exists and the user wants independent acceptance, strict signoff, or a skeptical QA gate before release.
The main need is a verdict plus evidence and defects, not implementation, polish, or builder-side re-testing.
avoid_when: The frontend still needs to be built, changed, or repaired rather than evaluated independently.
The request is ordinary browser implementation or builder-side QA that belongs with the team doing the web work.
requires: The browser-facing deliverable already exists and can be exercised independently.
A local canonical interactive browser QA reference for this skill family.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this nested child path before routing when the runtime cannot discover delivery-control children automatically.

children.name: compound
children.summary: Extracts reusable lessons and durable truths from completed work into docs/reference/lessons.md and docs/reference/memory.md. Rehydrates from live docs, session history, and existing archives before writing.
route_when: A feature, phase, or delivery cycle has completed and the team wants to capture lessons, decisions, or durable truths before context is lost.
A harness-design postflight hands off to compound extraction after orchestrated work finishes.
The user explicitly asks to update durable memory or extract lessons from recent work.
avoid_when: Work is still in progress and the need is implementation, orchestration, current-slice approval, or browser QA.
The request is generic note-taking, status reporting, or progress journaling rather than structured knowledge extraction.
The user wants a PRD, design spec, or architecture doc rather than extracted historical knowledge.
requires: Completed work with observable outcomes in live docs, session history, or conversation context.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this nested child path before routing when the runtime cannot discover delivery-control children automatically.
